word.item: the door doesn't want to stay shut
he was watching him (the old man)
caught up to and passed his master
the hollow place in the tree
do do re, so 2 or 3 times
in order to fly he wanted to go.
The horse is in the barn.
The mare is in the barn.
The ax is under the table.
I come out of the woods.
I wait to go into the woods.
He wants to go into the house.
I'm going down to the house.
The dog is playing in the house
The dog is playing in the house
g he/she didn't do that way
g it might be I'll come?
the snake is crawling ("slipping on the ground")
a little bit more I want
a man, when he got lost
a woman and a man go together
root used to make red powder
small ep. of white hickory, has small nuts
S thinks that it looks like the Sand-Jack Oak
I want to go down home
He was so old he couldn't see
He was so frightened, he couldn't run
I want to borrow your pirogue
he's so weak he can't run
bracelet worn on the upper arm
bracelet worn on the lower arm
bracelet worn on the lower arm
ornamental round pieces made of silver, gold
necklace of silver or gold pieces
my [parents], prob. also "ancestors," relatives, kin; my friend
something you wear on your head, like a handkerchief
silver or gold ornaments to wear on the head
silver or gold ornaments to wear on the head
he's putting on breechcloth, getting on drawers
my shoulder, back of the shoulder (shoulder-blade)
my child (little child or baby) (used by a man or a woman)
my grand-uncle, grandfather, parent, "my old parents"
boots that tie all the way up
I'm doing hard; I'm working hard
I'm not going to throw you in
a spider species having seven legs
I pass by and see you in your blood
he did it to me, + I pinch her
the people who were sitting were not dancing
